liaendmembers 0.3.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Endmembers

    Euclidean Distance for Pb Isotope rations to ore Sources

    euc_dist.Rd

    Calcutalte the culidian distanc of each isotope sample to a reference dataset, and gives the clossest regions to the groups. Mass-fractionation follows the procedure outlined in Albarede et.al (2024)

    euc_dist(x, ox, ox_col, group, .n = 10, as.dist = FALSE, ...)

mf_dist(x, ox, ox_col, group, .n = 10, as.dist = FALSE, s = 0.001, ...)

endmember_dist(
  x,
  ox,
  ox_col,
  group,
  dist_type = "ed",
  .n = 10,
  as.dist = FALSE,
  s = 0.001,
  ...
)

    Arguments

    x

    Matrix of Pb Isotopes with colums in the order of 206Pb/204Pb, 207Pb/204Pb,208Pb/204Pb or a `liaendmember` object.

    ox

    Dataframe of renfrence data contained the Pb Isotope rations

    ox_col

    Vector contained names of the Pb Isotope rations. Names much include digits 6, 7, 8 for isotope ratio identification.

    group

    Groups belonging to reach isotope ration vector

    .n

    Length of result output (Default = 10)

    as.dist

    Option to show distances or only a vector of closest groups.

    ...

    Additional params

    s

    Mass-fractionation factor (Defualt = 0.001)

    dist_type

    Distance type to use, simple euclidiant ('ed') or mass-fractionation corrected ('mfd')

    Value

    Data frame or charecter vector

    List of dataframe or charecter vector

    References

    Albarede, F., Davis, G., Blichert-Toft, J., Gentelli, L., Gitler, H., Pinto, M., & Telouk, P. (2024). A new algorithm for using Pb isotopes to determine the provenance of bullion in ancient Greek coinage. Journal of Archaeological Science, 163, 105919. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jas.2023.105919

    Contents
